SEARCH
Package com.gcu.controllers

Class CharactersController

java.lang.Object
com.gcu.controllers.CharactersController
@Controller public class CharactersController extends Object
Controller for handling character-related operations such as listing, viewing, creating, editing, and deleting characters. Also includes functionality for flagging/unflagging characters (admin only).

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    CharactersController(CharacterDatabaseService characterService, RaceDatabaseService raceService, ClassDatabaseService classService)
    Constructor for CharactersController, using dependency injection to receive service instances.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    characters(String keyword, String sortBy, String ownerPriority, org.springframework.ui.Model model, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
    Handles GET requests to "/characters" endpoint, allowing users to view a list of characters with optional search and sorting parameters.
    String
    createCharacter(CharacterEntity character, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
    Handles POST requests to "/characters/create" endpoint, allowing users to submit the form for creating a new character.

    Handles GET requests to "/characters/delete/{id}" endpoint, allowing users to delete an existing character by its ID.

    Handles POST requests to "/characters/flag/{id}" endpoint, allowing admin users to flag a character by its ID.

    Handles GET requests to "/characters/create" endpoint, allowing users to access the form for creating a new character.

    Handles GET requests to "/characters/edit/{id}" endpoint, allowing users to access the form for editing an existing character.

    Handles POST requests to "/characters/unflag/{id}" endpoint, allowing admin users to unflag a character by its ID.

    Handles POST requests to "/characters/edit" endpoint, allowing users to submit the form for editing an existing character.
    String
    viewCharacter(int id, org.springframework.ui.Model model, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
    Handles GET requests to "/characters/view/{id}" endpoint, allowing users to view details of a specific character by its ID.

  • Constructor Details

    • CharactersController

      public CharactersController(CharacterDatabaseService characterService, RaceDatabaseService raceService, ClassDatabaseService classService)
      Constructor for CharactersController, using dependency injection to receive service instances.
      Parameters:
      characterService - the service for managing character-related database operations
      raceService - the service for managing race-related database operations
      classService - the service for managing class-related database operations

  • Method Details

    • characters

      @GetMapping("/characters") public String characters(@RequestParam(value="keyword",required=false) String keyword, @RequestParam(value="sortBy",required=false) String sortBy, @RequestParam(value="ownerPriority",required=false) String ownerPriority, org.springframework.ui.Model model, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles GET requests to "/characters" endpoint, allowing users to view a list of characters with optional search and sorting parameters.
      Parameters:
      keyword - the search keyword for filtering characters by name (optional)
      sortBy - the sorting criteria for ordering characters (optional)
      ownerPriority - the owner priority filter for sorting characters (optional)
      model - the Model object to pass data to the
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and role
      Returns:
      the name of the view to render (characters.html)

    • viewCharacter

      @GetMapping("/characters/view/{id}") public String viewCharacter(@PathVariable int id, org.springframework.ui.Model model, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles GET requests to "/characters/view/{id}" endpoint, allowing users to view details of a specific character by its ID.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the character to view
      model - the Model object to pass data to the
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and role
      Returns:
      the name of the view to render (view-character.html) or a redirect to the characters list if the character is not found.

    • showCreateForm

      @GetMapping("/characters/create") public String showCreateForm(org.springframework.ui.Model model, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles GET requests to "/characters/create" endpoint, allowing users to access the form for creating a new character.
      Parameters:
      model - the Model object to pass data to the view
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication
      Returns:
      the name of the view to render (create-character.html) or a redirect to the sign-in page if the user is not authenticated.

    • createCharacter

      @PostMapping("/characters/create") public String createCharacter(@ModelAttribute("character") CharacterEntity character, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles POST requests to "/characters/create" endpoint, allowing users to submit the form for creating a new character. Validates the user's session and then adds the character to the database.
      Parameters:
      character - the CharacterEntity object populated from the form submission
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication
      Returns:
      a redirect to the characters list page after successful creation or a redirect to the sign-in page if the user is not authenticated.

    • showEditForm

      @GetMapping("/characters/edit/{id}") public String showEditForm(@PathVariable int id, org.springframework.ui.Model model, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles GET requests to "/characters/edit/{id}" endpoint, allowing users to access the form for editing an existing character. Validates the user's session and ownership of the character before allowing access to the edit form.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the character to edit
      model - the Model object to pass data to the
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and ownership of the character
      Returns:
      the name of the view to render (edit-character.html) or a redirect to the characters list if the character is not found or the user is not authorized to edit it.

    • updateCharacter

      @PostMapping("/characters/edit") public String updateCharacter(@ModelAttribute("character") CharacterEntity character, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles POST requests to "/characters/edit" endpoint, allowing users to submit the form for editing an existing character. Validates the user's session and ownership of the character before allowing the update to be saved to the database.
      Parameters:
      character - the CharacterEntity object populated from the form submission, including the character ID to identify which character to update
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and ownership of the character
      Returns:
      a redirect to the characters list page after successful update or a redirect to the sign-in page if the user is not authenticated.

    • deleteCharacter

      @GetMapping("/characters/delete/{id}") public String deleteCharacter(@PathVariable int id, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles GET requests to "/characters/delete/{id}" endpoint, allowing users to delete an existing character by its ID. Validates the user's session and ownership of the character before allowing the deletion from the database
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the character to delete
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and ownership of the character
      Returns:
      a redirect to the characters list page after successful deletion or a redirect to the characters list if the character is not found or the user is not authorized to delete it.

    • flagCharacter

      @PostMapping("/characters/flag/{id}") public String flagCharacter(@PathVariable int id, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles POST requests to "/characters/flag/{id}" endpoint, allowing admin users to flag a character by its ID. Validates the user's session and admin role before allowing the character to be flagged in the database.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the character to flag
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and admin role
      Returns:
      a redirect to the characters list page after successful flagging or a redirect to the characters list if the user is not an admin.

    • unflagCharacter

      @PostMapping("/characters/unflag/{id}") public String unflagCharacter(@PathVariable int id, jakarta.servlet.http.HttpSession session)
      Handles POST requests to "/characters/unflag/{id}" endpoint, allowing admin users to unflag a character by its ID. Validates the user's session and admin role before allowing the character to be unflagged in the database.
      Parameters:
      id - the ID of the character to unflag
      session - the HttpSession object to check for user authentication and admin role
      Returns:
      a redirect to the characters list page after successful unflagging or a redirect to the characters list if the user is not an admin.
